expanse of seething mud flats, beyond which rises an odd black monolith.
name: Atop a Large Crest

desc:
The land slowly slopes, equally divided between murky fens and small tree
covered mounds rising from the waters. The terrain begins to rise steeply to the
south and east; tall crags and ridges dominate the skyline. A large cleft to
your south looks intriguing, and perhaps might afford a safe place to scale the
ridge.
name: Forested Wetlands

desc:
The ground gets more wet going to the east, and in some places impossible to put
ones feet upon. Around the trees there is some drier terrain, which makes it
possible to carefully jump from one to another and proceed that way.
name: In a Swampy Forest

desc:
This is the last patch of pine before the land becomes too wet for it to grow
further in the east and south. Actually it looks like no trees whatsoever can
exist in the thick mist that fills the air in the valley to the east.
name: In a Pine Forest

desc:
A group of birches have found a good place in the fertile soil, and they have
grown tall and healthy to form this nice little grove. In the east the terrain
carefully descents to a misty valley, that does not look all that nice anymore.
name: A Birch Grove

desc:
An attempt has been made to block the trail to the north with branches, but it
does not really take much effort to remove the blockage. After that the trail
climbs up to a hill where it carries on. A small hill blocks the view in the
east as well.
name: On a Bending Trail

desc:
Little bushes have been ripped off from the side of the road and thrown on the
trail. Looks like some little animals have been feeding on the branches, they
are covered with toothprints. The trail slopes gently down in the south, where
it slightly bends westwards and disappears into the forest, but in the north it
follows the outline of the hills as long as you can see. East of here the plain
hills carry on endlessly.
name: A Sloping Trail

desc:
The hills seem to go on and on, without end. They are covered with little and
large rocks and the terrain is hard to walk on. Sharp shadows dance on the close
rock shapes as the wind sings in the cracks and valleys.
name: A Ridge of Hills

desc:
The terrain climbs up and down, making travelling rather difficult. As far as
one can see between the trunks, there seems to be some hills both in the west
and in the south.
name: Rising Forest

desc:
The trees in this part of the forest are not very old and the wood is rather
healthy. Maybe there was a fire that burned down the old forest and let a new
one grow on a good soil. There is a steep rise in the terrain to the east, and a
more gentle one to the south.
name: At Some Young Spruces

desc:
This gently forested hill gives a perfect view on the path in the north and over
the woods behind it. Far in the west you can see another trail running
north-south, and a huge tower near the crossroads of the two.
name: A Sloping Forest Hill

desc:
A steep slope rises to the west, filled with big lichen-covered rocks. One has
to watch his steps very carefully here to avoid slipping on the mossy ground.
Toward the east the wood changes slowly from spruce to a mix of deciduous trees.
name: East of a Forest Hill

desc:
Far and wide to the north the woodland stretches across the land. The treeline
lies only a few hundred yards below you, snaking its way along the flank of the
hill. Above the trees only a few hardy grasses and clumps of moss and heather
cling to the stony ground.
name: Bare Hillside

desc:
The hillside is covered with bushes and heather which thins out as the slope
rises to the south and west. Down the slope to the north, thick pines grow on
the side of the hill. To the east a spur of forest climbs higher up the hill,
coming almost level with your present vantage point. Many tiny flies burst from
any disturbed heather and cluster in the air around you.
name: A Flank of the Hillside

desc:
The trees struggle to cling to the ever increasing slope to the south. Here and
there a pine tree lies fallen downhill, its roots torn out from their tenacious
grasp by the ravages of the wind and rain. The lack of thick tree cover allows
many bushes and heather to flourish on the coarse sandy soil of the hillside.
name: Forest Covered Flank

desc:
A large landslide from the upper reaches of the hill to the south has formed
this gully in the slope. The slip must have occurred several years past as the
broken debris of rock and boulder is covered with lichens and moss. The sides of
the gully to the south, east and west are steep and fragile with outcrops of
loose rock and stone.
name: A Bare Gully

desc:
The ground climbs upward to the south toward a split between two hill summits.
To the north a large rockfall has created a precarious steep-sided gully down
the north face of the slope. The land crosses the saddle and drops away to the
south-east into a second valley in the hills.
name: A Sharply Rising Slope

desc:
The narrow ridge from which you now gaze runs roughly south-west to north-east
joining two high hill summits with a lower stretch of land, akin to a massive
hammock of rock and stone slung between the peaks. To the south-east the
hillside drops alarmingly downward into a steep river-cut valley.
name: A Saddle in the Hills

desc:
Bare hillside drops away on all sides of this lofty summit. The view of the
surrounding land is panoramic. Several hundred feet below to the north, east and
south, woods cover the land for miles around. To the west the range of hills
continues, its bare mass stretching away in a massive curve to the south-west.
This would be an excellent vantage point to behold any creatures travelling in
the lands below.
name: Crest of a Hill

desc:
You are perched on the broad north facing slope. To the south, the hill climbs
toward a peak and to the north, the ground drops away toward a dark green forest
of pines. A brave adventurer could continue to the east and west across the
rough rocky slope though walking would prove difficult on the damp uneven
surface.
name: High on a Hillside

desc:
The slope of the hillside reduces here with the ground almost level. To the
south-east the hills appear to rise to a summit. To the north the land drops
away from the plateau, becoming steep as it stretches down to the tree covered
plains beyond. Yellow and gray grasses and tough heather grow around you
harbouring a myriad of small flies and ticks.
name: Flattened Hilltop

desc:
The land rises steadily upward to the south. The barren hillside provides a hard
and unyielding footing with many boulders and rocks making progress slow and
wearying. To the north, trees cover the lower slopes, stretching over the
lowlands like a thick dark blanket.
name: Flank of the Hill

desc:
This hill provides a good view to the scenery. Though sparse in colours, the
forms of hill ranges and huge blocks of rock cast interesting shadows in the
terrain. One can almost see something big moving in the valleys.
name: On a Scenic Hill

desc:
A few bush there and some grass here, that is all that grows on the hillsides.
Tops of the hills have been cleared into blank rocky fields by the whining wind
and rains. In the northwest there is a descend down to the forests.
name: Ridge of Hills

desc:
A little opening in the forest leads to the foot of a vast hill range in the
south, where air is lighter, but life seems to be as hard as in the forests
north of here. The wind carries a sad crying voice from that way.
name: Forest by a Ridge of Hills

desc:
A tiny valley between two hills, one in the north and the other one in the
south, where all the trees disappear and all you can see that way is a long
ridge of hills. A little pond has formed here from the waters streaming down
from the south.
name: A Sloping Forest

desc:
The hills seem to go on and on, without end. They are covered with little and
large rocks and the terrain is hard to walk on. Sharp shadows dance on the close
rock shapes as the wind sings in the cracks and valleys.
name: A Ridge of Hills

desc:
The hills seem to go on and on, without end. They are covered with little and
large rocks and the terrain is hard to walk on. Sharp shadows dance on the close
rock shapes as the wind sings in the cracks and valleys.
name: A Ridge of Hills

desc:
The hills seem to go on and on, without end. They are covered with little and
large rocks and the terrain is hard to walk on. Sharp shadows dance on the close
rock shapes as the wind sings in the cracks and valleys.
name: A Ridge of Hills

desc:
The hills seem to go on and on, without end. They are covered with little and
large rocks and the terrain is hard to walk on. Sharp shadows dance on the close
rock shapes as the wind sings in the cracks and valleys.
name: A Ridge of Hills

desc:
The ground levels out and dips slightly around you. This natural hollow provides
shelter on the otherwise exposed hillside. The slight protection from the
elements allows a few coarse shrubs and grasses to cling to life amid the had
grey rocks and boulders. The hilly land continues all around with the summit of
a large hill to the east.
name: Sheltered Plateau

desc:
The shrubs and bushes of the lower slopes give way to hardy heathers and lichens
growing where they can amidst the hard and unyielding ground. To the east, the
land looks to rise several hundred feet to the summit among the hills. On this
bleak hillside, your passage is obvious to any sharp eyed creature abroad.
name: Below the Crest

desc:
The rough boulder strewn hillside drops away alarmingly to the south and east. A
steep sided valley cuts into the hillside to the south and you can discern a
small stream tumbling down the vale. To the north the hill climbs upward toward
its summit, hundreds of feet above you.
name: Below the Hilltop

desc:
The broad back of the hill lies flat here with several hundreds of yards of
almost level ground stretching in all directions. Further to the east and north,
the hillside climbs upward toward a summit. To the south the land drops toward
the trollshaws. Few signs of wildlife are visible on this barren plateau.
name: High Plateau

desc:
The rough hard ground descends to the south, leading down out of the high hills
to the north. To the east and west the high ground continues along the range of
hills. Gazing out over the lowland to the south, the trollshaws stretch far and
wide, a blight upon the land.
name: Barren Hillside

desc:
Down here in the valley the wind does not blow so cold, but the whine of it can
still be heard above. The valley continues in the west, where it enters a dank
misty area. Hills in north and south are steep, but easily climbable.
name: Between Two Hills

desc:
A huge cliff rises high in the north and makes travelling that way impossible.
The ascent in the south is much more friendly even if the hill that way is at
least comparable to the one in north. The valley to the west looks cold and wet,
and nothing can be seen through the fog.
name: Entering a Misty Valley

desc:
Just barely through the mist you can see the rising hills in the north,
apparently a rather vast hill range starts there. To the east, toward a small
pass going further east, the air gets lighter.
name: Edge of a Misty Valley

desc:
At least a little guidance out of this fog provides the rocky cliff that looks
too hard to climb in the north. It might be possible to find cracks to put your
feet on up there, but they cannot be seen from here.
name: North of a Misty Valley

desc:
The hill range does not go any further south, instead there is a steady decline
after which the terrain is swallowed under thick field of fog. The large valley
in the south-east is all covered in fog and the smell of decaying wood is clear
in the air.
name: Lowering Hills

desc:
The frightening mist deepens east of here, flowing like a living being waiting
for the unwary traveller. The birch grove in west looks totally out of place
next to it.
name: Edge of a Valley

desc:
Apparently this is the southwest corner of the valley, at least the mist is not
so thick in this direction. The terrain still descends a bit to the south, where
it also gets wet and swampy.
name: Corner of a Misty Valley

desc:
The water from the surrounding forest seem to collect here, and disappear down
through this little hole. Not much can be said about where it then goes, only
distant plops can be heard down there. Only way to move here without soaking
ones pants is to jump from tree root to another, hoping not to slip.
name: In a Swampy Forest

desc:
Rising from the bogs and mire, these rounded knolls are densely blanketed with a
multitude of fragrant evergreens, offsetting the suspect odour wafting from the
swamplands. Under the protective boughs of firs and pines are a multitude of
tiny flowers--white and lavender and violet. A sheer bluff dominates the eastern
horizon, only a fool would attempt to ascend it. However, a small animal path
winds upwards to the south.
name: Thickly-Wooded Knolls

desc:
Luckily for you, the local fauna has established a faint trail along the hills,
otherwise undoubtably you would have broken your neck ascending these bleak and
treacherous uplands. Bare rocky slopes nuture but a few battered trees straining
with life's tenacious will to survive in these infertile lands where the wind
howls incessantly. Lichen and an odd reddish moss sprout from cracks or
sheltered niches in the rocks, enlivening the otherwise gray and disenheartening
surroundings. The trail spilts to the east or winds up and down the ascending
ridgeface.
name: Along a Precipitous Trail

desc:
A game trail winds from below, to a small saddle between the hills, then
branches west and north, rising sharply to the west as it runs along a steep
ridge of exposed rock. A few scattered trees nestle on the leeward slopes, firs
and scrub pines, but otherwise the vegetation is scanty.
name: An Ascending Ridge

desc:
The earth here is only semi-solid; a muddy mire that only barely supports ones
weight. Nothing grows here, and the whole area smells of brimstone. To the east
the mud grows soupy, and gas and water bubble from small fumaroles. A slippery,
muddy slope rises to the north...it should be possible for an experienced
climber to navigate the slope however.
name: Noxious Mud Flats

desc:
Cauldrons of boiling mud simmer sullenly, strange off-white plumes of mineral-
laden steam rising in languid spirals to generate a strange mist that curls and
cools, redissolving sulfurous compounds in a crusty rime that covers all
available surfaces. The whole of the land lies choked in self-destroying
paroxysms, rending itself, bleeding from crystalline pustules. A horrific
malodourous stench of death and decay assaults the senses, an indescribable
amalgamation of charnel putrefaction, fermenting offal, and plague-ridden
purulence.
name: Sulfurous Mud Pits

desc:
A shelf of bluish-grey limestone rises above the immediate landscape, its
surface well blanketed with tall pines and firs, and an occasional oak tree.
Golden thistles bloom among rough grasses and small fragrant plants. The plateau
drops off suddenly to the southwest, but you detect a small game trail to your
north, that while best suited for goats, looks climbable.
name: A Forested Plateau

desc:
While more suited for goats than humanoids, a faint trail weaves its way along
the side of a ridge then down to a shelf of bluish limestone a goodly distance
below you. With care and paitence one might make their way through the hills
northward, or follow the the goat-path along the course of the setting sun.
name: Desolate Highlands

desc:
Spindly windswept pines cling to the rocky surface, their vitality sapped by
vines of broomrape curled vampirically about their trunks and limbs. The
highlands fall away suddenly to the northwest, revealling a high cliff-face
overlooking pine-dominated forests. The environ is quiet and desolate, and a
sense of calm stillness pervades the air. Still, it does not feel like the calm
of peace but rather that of grim expectation.
name: Forested Bluff

desc:
As the result of a small rise in the terrain, the ground gets drier, and at the
same time trees grow closer to each other. To the east the spruce forest slowly
becomes dominated by straight pines. In the west, where the little streams seems
to lead, the terrain becomes swampy.
name: Deepening Swamp Forest

desc:
A horrifying darkness lies in the valley to the north. The air is still and wet,
cold to the bones. The little one can see in other directions, there seems to be
a little hill in the east.
name: South Edge of a Misty Valley

desc:
In middle of the fog you can only see a few yards away before everything is
effectively blocked by the moist air flowing here.
name: Deep in a Misty Valley

desc:
Only way one can tell the valley ends here is that the ground leaves up in the
east, until it becomes too steep and rocky to continue. Moving carefully it
could be possible to follow the edge of hills southwards without losing oneself
in the fog.
name: Edge of a Valley

desc:
This hill provides excellent view over the misty valley opening in the west. It
is very hard to say how far down there the ground really is, or if there is
ground at all. The flowing air here is moisty and smells like rotten wood and
fungi. In the north the hill slopes down to a small pass going east-west.
name: View to a Valley

desc:
The slope slowly makes it way up toward west, where it seems to become one of
the highest points in the visible scenery. Ground here is covered with sharp
edged rocks that make walking slow and dangerous, because each step must be
thought up before proceeding.
name: A Sloping Hill

desc:
The hillside drops steeply to the south-west, leading down into a densely wooded
valley. To the north and east the hillside rises upward, climbing the flanks of
the range of hills. The view to the south encompasses the forest and plains of
the trollshaws.
name: Rim of a Vale

desc:
The hillside rolls downward to the south and east. High on the slope, the
vegetation is sparse and thin but as the land descends more tufts of grass and
heathers appear on the rocky ground. The broad forests of the trollshaws lie
spread out on the lowlands several miles to the south-east.
name: Broad Expanse of Hillside

desc:
The long spur of high ground from which you gaze, stretches out to the south-
east like a massive finger. To the north-east a narrow valley cuts deeply into
the hillside, its steep sides impassable to any creature without the power of
flight. To the south and the east, the ridge continues.
name: Along a Ridge

desc:
A long outcrop of hillside runs north-west to south-east, looming over the
trollshaws. To the north, you can climb to the top of the ridge. To the south
the slope becomes too steep to negotiate easily. The ridge continues to the east
and west, its surface of loose rock and scree providing a slippery and dangerous
footing.
name: South Face of a Ridge

desc:
A rocky ridge rears above you to the north, cutting across the land from the
northwest to the southeast. Its upper levels are denuded of trees, perhaps
bearing too little soil to support such life. But here, on its lower slopes, a
number of hardy pine trees seem to thrive, though further to the east, where the
ground descends toward a deep gully, they again thin out and disappear.
name: A Steep and Wooded Slope

desc:
You are in a thick wood composed mainly of dark, bushy trees. Interspersed in
the forest every now and then are some small pine trees, spindly and weak. To
the south and west the woodland continues, uninterrupted, but to the north it
thins out a little, as it climbs a steep slope. Eastward the ground makes an
abrupt plunge into a deep little gully, and there is a break in the trees.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
The thick woods continue here, the atmosphere dense and imposing. The trees here
are not ordered and friendly, like in a pine forest, but seem to grasp at the
sky with gnarled branches. To the west and north the dense forest continues,
while to the east the ground plunges off into a deep gully, which provides a
break in the trees.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
Tall trees loom above you, their boughs spreading to form a lofty roof above
your head. About their bases grow a variety of bushes and shrubs, wildflowers
and weeds, forming a rich, varied and dense undergrowth, and scenting the air
with sweet perfumes, which mingle with the redolent odour of pines. To the east
the land slopes away from you, and the trees thin out towards a fairly deep
depression.
name: In a Thick Forest

desc:
The woods here are densely packed with thick bushy fir trees. Occasionally you
see a thin spindly pine tree, fighting with the others for water and light. To
the north, east and south the woods remain much the same, being quite dense and
fairly dim. But to the west they seem to become lighter and rather more open.
name: A Forest of Firs and Pines

desc:
The thick forest surrounding you is made of densely packed fir trees. The trees
have dark green foliage, and are growing closely together. To the north and east
you can see nothing but more forest, while to the west you think you can make
out some kind of clearing.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
You find yourself in a thick forest of dense, bushy trees. Only a little natural
light can penetrate the foliage of the canopy overhead. The woodland stretches
out in all directions, but to the north you can see that the ground climbs
markedly, whilst to the east it begins a very gradual descent.
name: Dense Woods

desc:
A vast, rocky ridge runs from the northwest to the southeast in this area,
looming high above the surrounding land. Here, toward its base, it still
supports a good deal of vegetation, and even a few hardy pine trees, which
thicken out as you descend to the south and west. Eastward the land remains
quite sloped, climbing to the northeast, but descending in the opposite
direction, toward a distant gully.
name: Southwest Face of a Ridge

desc:
With a nigh uncanny precision, the trees of the lower valley end in a straight
line across the sloping hillside. Many small bushes and shrubs grow above the
treeline and rise away from you for several hundred feet. From a bush near you
comes a strange murmuring buzz.
name: Above the Treeline

desc:
The ground drops away sharply to the west into the uppermost reach of a valley.
The trees begin a few hundred yards below you though many small bushes and
shrubs grow higher, almost to your vantage point. The hard rocky hillside climbs
upward to the north and east and you can continue along the face of the slope to
the south.
name: Head of a Valley

desc:
The valley is sheltered on the north and east sides by the rising hillside
beyond. This natural protection has allowed the wood and brush to proliferate
and the thick undergrowth makes travel difficult. To the south and west the
valley descends slightly.
name: In a Thickly Wooded Valley

desc:
Less and less trees grow on the sloping hills, and in the north a vast region
opens without a single tree bar a few puny bushes. Cold wind blows from that
way, but the big pines quickly decay it into whine in the canopy.
name: In a Pine Forest

desc:
The terrain gets even higher in the north, rising to a tall hill. Clearly one of
the most visible landmarks of the region, it effectively blocks your view
further north. The lower parts of the trees here have been marked by some
animal, an animal with big claws.
name: Forest South of a Hill

desc:
The ground drops a few feet down in the west, where it steadily continues to
descend until the fog hides it from the view. The trees are mainly pine here,
with a few small birches in between.
name: Forest before a Valley

desc:
A few feet high cliff separates the two levels of the forest from each other,
and a small waterwall brings the water from the upper level down to a stream
that continues westward. It is possible to climb the cliff up, as long as you
remember to choose the steps carefully.
name: Cliff in a Pine Forest

desc:
The pines grow close together, and the canopy does not let much light to the
ground level. A little stream collects several tiny ones from the north, and
turns the course of the water west toward a cliff edge.
name: Dark Pine Forest

desc:
The ground is dry here, and only some grass grows on it. The pines seem to
thrive in the sandy soil though, perhaps there is enough water underground for
them to have a good life here.
name: Dark Pine Forest

desc:
Many differing types of trees grow in this sheltered valley. The enclosing
hillside and thick trees combine to cut down any wind to naught but a whisper. A
few chitterings and squeaks of woodland creatures can be heard from beneath the
foliage. The trees continue to the north and west. To the east and south, steep
hillside rises up above you.
name: Tree Covered Valley

desc:
Tall pines and firs grow all about you, and at their bases a variety of bushes
and shrubs make up an equally healthy undergrowth. Westward the trees thin out
markedly, giving way to an open area, and to the north another clearing can be
seen. South and east the trees continue, if anything growing thicker.
name: In a Thin Forest

desc:
The forest parts and a large expanse of meadow is revealed. While trees do not
grow here, there is a large variety of small plants, shrubs, and flowers that
seem to enjoy the soil and sunlight. However, there is a large area in the
centre of the meadow with no grass or vegetation at all, and there is a circle
of blackened stones placed amidst it.
name: Space between the Trees

desc:
A large glade opens amid the forest, with large areas of grassy meadows
interspersed among the trees. Here and there are bunches of tiny white blossoms
growing in low humps among the grass. The forest is shrouded with gloom to the
northwest, but in the other direction the glade widens to form a large space
open to the sun.
name: Patches of Trees

desc:
The ravine contines to wind along to the west of this gloomy, darkened forest.
Trees lock branches, preventing the light from penetrating to the earth where
low broad-capped mushrooms and tiny white flowers spring from the moist soil.
name: Murky Evergreen Forest

desc:
The forest opens up into a small clearing. Scattered about are rotting,
splintered logs that were evidently at one time part of an old cabin. Now,
however there is no sign of human inhabitants, just the silent pines standing
vigilantly among patches of heather and bramble. Everywhere you look fragrant
evergreens rise from the landscape, with the sharp crest of a ridge peeping from
the treetops to your northwest.
name: Near a Ramshackle Building

desc:
The ground arches upward towards the high ridge to your north. Grand, tall pines
and blue spruces find a comfortable existence in the stony but fairly fertile
soil. Small rounded bushes and tufts of grass grow between the trees, healthy if
not prolific. A vaguely delineated trail weaves it way along the edge of the
ridge, following the arching stone upwards.
name: Rising Evergreen Forest

desc:
A wide ridge of hills arches above the landscape to your south, affording a view
of spacious forested lands. Pines and an occasional fir or larch surround you,
with small patches of brush, grass, and lichen underfoot. To the northeast the
forest becomes dim and unappealing, below you espy a path winding down the
cliff-face.
name: A Forested Ridge

desc:
Darkly twisted, knotted pines grow in large clusters on any available flat
surfaces. Close around you brambles creep in and around the trees; the air is
musty and conveys a sense of unease. Forest continues in all directions, south
there is a sheer dropoff overlooking a wide band of forests.
name: Clusters of Pines

desc:
The forest begins to transition here, growing dense and dim to your north and
thinning and sloping downward towards the rising sun. Pines rise prolifically
all about you, giving the air a brisk clean smell.
name: Tree-Laden Slope

desc:
A fairly broad valley bottom is defined by the steep slopes to the east and to
the west. Climbing gently northward, the vale gradually curves away to the east,
in the far distance, but the only way to discern this is by spying the hilltops,
for the entire area is populated by tall and healthy trees, which thrive in this
sheltered and apparently well-watered area.
name: Climbing the Valley Floor

desc:
The ground rises sharply to the east, and only a little less so to the west,
defining a valley bottom, which itself climbs gently northwards. The entire area
is densely packed with trees, pines and firs standing shoulder to shoulder with
a variety of deciduous species, making a rich mixed woodland.
name: In a Thickly Wooded Vale

desc:
You have entered a small clearing in the woods. Covering the ground are small
bushy shrubs with broad green leaves. Small animals scurry around beneath these
bushes. On all sides the trees rise about you, but whilst to the north, the east
and the west the woodland seems quite thick, to the south it remains rather
open.
name: A Wide Clearing

desc:
A notable stand of tall firs and birches springs from a low rise, dominating the
local skyline. Some dry brush and tinder are spread under the evergreens, which
thin and disappear to your south, opening into a wide clearing.
name: Stand of Fir and Birch

desc:
A pale mist redolent of rotting vegetation casts an unsettling pall about the
forest. Indeed, the trees here seem rather unhealthy and discoloured somehow,
with a strange greyish cast to their leaves and branches. Strange parasitic
brambles curl up and down the trees to the exclusion of any other plant growth.
A large impassable cliffwall rises to the north.
name: Dismal Forest

desc:
Irregularly shaped clusters of trees grow amidst low grasses. Pines and firs
predominate, with round mounds of brush scattered underfoot. A steeply sloped
but not impassable trail twists up through the trees.
name: Evergreen Forest

desc:
A very large space between two hills seems as if it has been cloven by some
powerful force, be it natural or unnatural. A small path leads up to a shelf,
where a small promontory offers a good view of the wetlands to the north, and
evergreen woodlands to the west, beyond which you see a tiny ribbon that appears
to be an established trail.
name: Cleft in the Hills

desc:
The ceiling and floor of the cave look like naturally formed, probably some of
the ground has been carried away by rain but the thick network of tree roots has
made new undergrowth grow on top of it, thus leaving this opening down here.
Some trunks have been carried to make walls and give extra support to the
ceiling.
name: A Natural Cave

desc:
Almost rotten wooden steps make their way up to something that looks like a
bigger room. The stonewalls are also covered with moss. A rather awful stench of
rotten meat is coming from downstairs.
name: Stairway

desc:
The round room houses one small table with some dirty equipment on it, as well
as one bed, some chairs and something moving in the shadows. A floortrap in the
floor has disappeared so there is just a square hole now. Another hole can be
seen in the ceiling, probably a place for a ladder. Several little windows line
the walls.
name: Guardroom

desc:
This is just a small hole in the ceiling of a round room. A ladder has been set
in it to access the top of the tower.
name: Ladder

desc:
A big bird's nest between two branches makes climbing higher impossible, but the
view is good enough from here. Especially in the south.
name: Up an Old Pine

desc:
A foul odour cloys the air of this dark cavern. The dark black walls sweat with
cold clammy moisture and even the tiniest sound echoes around the void. The
floor of the cave is littered with rubble and shattered white bone fragments of
long dead creatures. Dried black gore lies hardened onto the rock of the cave
floor, a dark stain on the grey rock. The cavern extends at least forty feet
into the heart of the hillside. At the very back of the cave, hidden in shadows,
a dark hole gapes in the roof.
name: A Stinking Cavern

desc:
Dried blood smears the floor and walls of this stinking lair. Many bones lie
scattered around, excreted undigested from the creature which consumed the
carcass. The black rock of the walls are cold and damp with moisture leaching
through the living rock of the hillside. Much of the floor is covered with a
dried droppings and a horrid stench grasps at your throat.
name: A Gory Cave

desc:
The ledge from which you now gaze lies at least one hundred feet above the
forest floor far below. The cliff drops vertically from the ledge offering no
handholds on its sheer face. The ledge juts out no more than five feet square, a
crumbling blemish on the cliff face. Far and wide below, the forests of the
trollshaws stretch out like a ragged green blanket.
name: A Precarious Ledge

desc:
A flat, broad elevated area looms above the hills to the south. The floor of
this plateau is covered by sand and twigs, rubble and dust. Sloping downwards a
tad, this flat area stretches further on to the east. Climbing the cliff to
reach the hills south of here would be impossible. Some hills with flat tops lie
to the north and a tall hill rises off the ground west of here; reaching its top
from here is unfeasible unless flying.
name: Dusty Plateau

desc:
Dry air fills the cellar and dust rises from the floor as you step on it. Still
it looks less decayed than the ruins on the surface, like some invisible hand
would have been fixing it up every now and then. Surely the wooden pillars by
the western wall have been just recently put there to keep the roof up.
name: A Fallen Cellar

desc:
The tunnel has been smoothly carved into the sandy soil and the flat rocks on
the sides keep the walls and roof up, along with a few wooden support pillars.
North end of the tunnel is blocked with a metal door, painted to look like a
stone. In the west the tunnels descend down to some wider space.
name: A Hidden Tunnel

desc:
The cave comes to an end here, leaving the only obvious exit out the tunnel in
the east wall. It might also be safer to stay there, since the wall here has
crumbled down in places, and probably will not take many years until the whole
cave comes down.
name: A Secret Cave

desc:
The land slopes upward from south to north, gradually at first then rising
steeply into dim highlands. Trees huddle somewhat close together all along the
incline, thinning slowly to the south and west. The earth is rocky and in the
main barren; only a few hardy weeds and lichens find a home among the broken
stones.
name: Rising Woodlands

desc:
The wood here is a strange combination of birch and spruce. Birds can be heard
singing their song up in the trees, where they probably are nesting. In the
south-east all the trees disappear before a long ridge of brush-covered hills.
name: Under Mixed Trees

desc:
A dark forest of firs and pines looms all about you, the forms of trees shadowy
and indistinct only a short distance away. Small bushy plants grow amid the
trees and purplish vines snake up the tree trunks, with vampiric suckers locked
into the bark. Trees can be seen in every direction, but seem to thin somewhat
to the east.
name: Dimly-lit Forest

desc:
There is not a single tree on these hills, so the undergrowth is very thick and
makes moving rather difficult. All the time one has to change his way to avoid
running into thorny bushes. Down on the west side of the hills there is a light
forest.
name: Hills by a Mixed Forest

desc:
The eastern end of a large cave opens up here. The floor consists of flat rocks
on the sand, and a heavy layer of dust starts to dance on them as you step from
one stone to another. The walls are completely made up of stones, indicating
that the hall might have served some greater purpose than a hideout sometime.
name: A Secret Cave

desc:
A strange odour hangs in the hair, catching at the back of your throat as you
breath. The ground is devoid of vegetation or animal tracks and is stained a
deep black colour. Here and there, pieces of rusted metal stick up from the
ground and the scattered sun-bleached bones of some long dead creatures lie
forgotten on the ground.
name: Bleak Glade

desc:
A few beams of light filter in through tiny cracks and holes in what was
formerly the floor of an old cabin. Now nothing remains of the former
inhabitants but some old refuse and debris scattered about. You detect some
sinister movement in the shadows however...perhaps there are some new denizens
of this abode?
name: The Basement
